seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Llama Stack enables real-time conversational AI using Meta's standardized interface for safety, memory, and tools. Guardrails are essential due to the high-risk nature of voice interactions, where false positives in safety filtering, latency violations, or incoherent responses can degrade user experience or cause harm. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure reliable, safe performance in sub-second latency pipelines.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents follow a real-time pipeline: speech-to-text (STT) transcribes audio to text, Llama Stack's agentic loop handles reasoning with safety shields, memory retrieval, and tool calls, then text-to-speech (TTS) synthesizes responses. Llama Stack's SDK manages conversational state, applying safety filters and memory banks per turn. The challenge is maintaining sub-second end-to-end latency while ensuring coherent, safe outputs.

Key components include the LlamaStackClient for agent turns, integrated with STT services like Whisper and TTS like Bark or ElevenLabs. Each agent turn is wrapped with Vex verification to catch failures like safety false positives or schema mismatches before TTS. Memory retrieval from vector stores must be precise to avoid hallucinated context in voice flows.

This architecture demands tight integration: STT → Llama Stack agent.verify() → Vex verify() → TTS, with latency budgets enforced at each stage.

risks.name: Safety shield false positives
risks.description: Llama Stack's safety filters block benign voice queries like medical symptom discussions
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies response appropriateness post-safety filter with contextual voice intent

risks.name: Latency budget exceeded
risks.description: Agent turns + tools exceed 500ms, breaking real-time voice interaction
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces strict latency SLAs per pipeline stage

risks.name: Conversation incoherence
risks.description: Memory retrieval hallucinates irrelevant context, causing disjointed voice dialogue
risks.mitigation: Vex validates memory associations against conversation history

risks.name: Tool schema violations
risks.description: Tool responses don't match expected JSON schemas, crashing voice pipeline
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation on all tool outputs before TTS

implementation.title: Basic Llama Stack Voice Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize LlamaStackClient with safety and memory enabled for voice pipeline
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackClient
import asyncio

client = LlamaStackClient(
    model="llama-3.2-3b",
    safety_config={'enabled': True, 'categories': ['hate', 'violence']},
    memory_config={'enabled': True, 'embedding_model': 'text-embedding-3-small'}
)

async def voice_agent_turn(audio_text: str, conversation_id: str) -> str:
    response = await client.chat.completions.create(
        messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': audio_text}],
        conversation_id=conversation_id,
        stream=False,
        max_turns=3
    )
    return response.choices[0].message.content

implementation.title: STT → Llama Stack → TTS Pipeline
implementation.description: Real-time pipeline integrating Whisper STT with Llama Stack agent turns
implementation.code:
import whisper
import elevenlabs
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackClient

stt = whisper.load_model('tiny')
client = LlamaStackClient(model="llama-3.2-3b")
voice = elevenlabs.Voice.from_id('your_voice_id')

async def voice_pipeline(audio_bytes: bytes, conversation_id: str) -> bytes:
    # STT
    text = stt.transcribe(audio_bytes)['text']
    
    # Llama Stack agent
    response_text = await voice_agent_turn(text, conversation_id)
    
    # TTS
    audio_response = voice.synthesize(response_text)
    return audio_response

implementation.title: Tool Integration with Schema Validation
implementation.description: Define and call tools within Llama Stack with strict schema enforcement
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List

class WeatherToolResponse(BaseModel):
    temperature: float
    condition: str
    location: str

async def get_weather(city: str) -> WeatherToolResponse:
    # Mock weather API
    return WeatherToolResponse(
        temperature=72.5,
        condition='sunny',
        location=city
    )

# Register tool with Llama Stack
client.register_tool(
    name='get_weather',
    description='Get current weather for a city',
    parameters={'city': {'type': 'string'}},
    handler=get_weather
)

implementation.title: Memory Bank for Conversation Context
implementation.description: Store and retrieve conversation history with embedding similarity search
implementation.code:
async def store_conversation_memory(client, conversation_id: str, message: str):
    await client.memory.store(
        conversation_id=conversation_id,
        content=message,
        metadata={'timestamp': '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z', 'role': 'user'}
    )

async def retrieve_context(client, conversation_id: str, query: str, top_k: int = 3):
    memories = await client.memory.search(
        conversation_id=conversation_id,
        query=query,
        top_k=top_k,
        threshold=0.8
    )
    return [m.content for m in memories]

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Agent Turns with Vex Safety Verification
vexIntegration.description: Verify safety filter outputs don't block benign voice content

vexIntegration.title: Latency Budget Enforcement
vexIntegration.description: Ensure sub-second response times for real-time voice requirements
vexIntegration.code:
import time
import vex

@vex.verify(
    checks=[
        vex.latency_check(max_latency_ms=500),
        vex.custom_check('end_to_end_latency', lambda ctx: ctx['total_time'] < 0.8)
    ]
)
async def low_latency_voice_pipeline(audio_bytes: bytes, conversation_id: str):
    start_time = time.time()
    
    text = stt.transcribe(audio_bytes)['text']
    response_text = await safe_voice_turn(text, conversation_id)
    audio_out = voice.synthesize(response_text)
    
    total_latency = time.time() - start_time
    return {
        'audio': audio_out,
        'total_time': total_latency,
        'stt_time': stt_times,
        'llm_time': llm_times
    }
